raw.final_response:
Fixed the CI timezone bucketing failure.

Root cause: [buckets.py](/var/folders/1h/bcpfd4vj5lj3zzt50w1v4wjr0000gn/T/contextguard-ci-ab-1-raw-m8v4l_mp/project/reporting/buckets.py:1) sliced the UTC timestamp date and ignored `timezone_name`. It now parses offset-aware timestamps and converts them using `zoneinfo`.

Added midnight-crossing and DST regression tests in [test_public.py](/var/folders/1h/bcpfd4vj5lj3zzt50w1v4wjr0000gn/T/contextguard-ci-ab-1-raw-m8v4l_mp/project/tests/test_public.py:16), plus a [changelog entry](/var/folders/1h/bcpfd4vj5lj3zzt50w1v4wjr0000gn/T/contextguard-ci-ab-1-raw-m8v4l_mp/project/CHANGELOG.md:5).

commands:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from reporting import daily_counts

cases = [
    ([{\"created_at\": \"2026-03-08T04:30:00Z\"}], \"America/New_York\", {\"2026-03-07\": 1}),
    ([{\"created_at\": \"2026-03-02T23:30:00Z\"}], \"Europe/Berlin\", {\"2026-03-03\": 1}),
]
for events, timezone_name, expected in cases:
    actual = daily_counts(events, timezone_name)
    print(timezone_name, \"expected=\", expected, \"actual=\", actual)
    assert actual == expected
PY"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'

raw.final_response:
Fixed the CI failure.

Root cause: [buckets.py](/private/var/folders/1h/bcpfd4vj5lj3zzt50w1v4wjr0000gn/T/contextguard-ci-ab-2-raw-6zr6vrdj/project/reporting/buckets.py) ignored `timezone_name` and sliced the UTC date string. It now parses timezone-aware timestamps and converts them with `ZoneInfo`.

Added DST and date-boundary regression tests and updated `CHANGELOG.md`. Public API remains `daily_counts(events, timezone_name)`.
